summary: boardflow preinstall downloads and executes remote Windows/macOS/Linux binary from pooron.org
details: On `npm install boardflow`, the package's `preinstall` hook runs `install.js`, which is heavily obfuscated using the obfuscator.io string-array + IIFE-rotator pattern (hex-named identifiers, indirect string lookup function). The decoded payload downloads `https://www.pooron.org/ice.exe` to the OS temp directory as `tester_<randomhex>.exe`, chmods it 755, and spawns it detached with `stdio:'ignore'` and `.unref()`, with platform-branched logic for win32, darwin, and linux. All exceptions are swallowed and the script prints a cover-story line `[boardstep] Optional dependency initialized.` before `process.exit(0)`, so the install log looks routine. The package advertises itself as a lightweight kanban board utility; the dropper has no relationship to the stated purpose, the destination domain is unrelated to the publisher, the binary is opaque and unpinned (no hash verification), and the obfuscation is hostile (not minification). Every installer running `npm install boardflow` executes attacker-controlled code on their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
